violations.text: -Baby Formula was observed held past expiration date. Baby Formula was voluntarily disposed of during time of inspection. -Onions were observed moldy and unwholesome to sight. Food was voluntarily disposed of. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 09/05/2025

Domesticated feline animal and food and water were observed in establishment during time of inspection. Live animals may not be allowed on the premises of a food establishment. PIC informed. ADA guidelines on service animals provided during time of inspection. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 09/05/2025

Beverage shelving in walk-in cooler was observed lined with cardboard. Cardboard was observed with green and white residue. This material is not easily cleanable. PIC instructed to cease use. New Violation. Correct By: 09/05/2025
The door to the toilet room is not self-closing. New Violation. Correct By: 09/19/2025
Mops must be allowed to air dry after use. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 09/05/2025

The ceiling tiles in the deli area are constructed of composite acoustic tiles. This material is not durable. Materials for ceiling surfaces shall be smooth, durable, easily cleanable and non-absorbent for areas subject to moisture such as food preparation areas, walk-in refrigerators, ware washing areas, and toilet rooms. New Violation. Correct By: 01/30/2025

Deceased mice observed on traps (glue and snap) during time of inspection. Trapped pests to be removed at a frequency to prevent becoming attractants to other pests. Corrected On-Site. Repeat Violation. Correct By: 11/14/2024

The ceiling tiles in the deli area are constructed of composite acoustic tiles. This material is not durable. Materials for wall surfaces shall be smooth, durable, easily cleanable, and non-absorbent for areas subject to moisture such as food preparation areas, walk-in refrigerators, ware-washing areas, and toilet rooms. New Violation. Correct By: 01/30/2025
Floors under retail shelving have an accumulation of soil residue and debris. Physical facilities must be maintained clean. New Violation. Correct By: 11/17/2024
Packaged cookies were observed in direct contact with rodent droppings. Food was voluntarily disposed of. Corrected On-Site. Repeat Violation. Correct By: 10/29/2024

1. The presence of cockroaches is not controlled as evidenced by live insects observed during time of inspection and deceased cockroaches observed on glue traps. 2. The presence of rodents is not controlled as evidence by rodent droppings on the floor and rodent droppings on retail shelving. The presence of insects, rodents, and other pests must be controlled by routinely inspecting the premises for evidence of pests, using acceptable methods to control pests, eliminating harborage conditions, and routinely inspecting incoming shipments of food and supplies. Deceased pests must be removed at a frequency to prevent attractants for other pests. Establishment must work with commercial pest control services to eliminate pest issues. Repeat Violation. Correct By: 10/15/2024

The door to the outside, located in the front, is not tight-fitting. Outer openings of a food establishment must be protected against the entry of insects and rodents by tight-fitting doors. Repeat Violation. Correct By: 10/29/2024

The non-food contact surfaces of the retail shelving have an accumulation of apparent rodent droppings. Shelving was actively being cleaned during time of inspection. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 10/29/2024

The floors under 3 bay sink and near grease trap have an accumulation of soil residue and food debris. The physical facilities shall be cleaned as often as necessary to keep them clean. Repeat Violation. Correct By: 10/29/2024

The ceiling tiles in the deli area are constructed of composite acoustic tiles. This material is not durable. Materials for wall surfaces shall be smooth, durable, easily cleanable, and non-absorbent for areas subject to moisture such as food preparation areas, walk-in refrigerators, ware-washing areas, and toilet rooms. New Violation. Correct By: 01/30/2025

Unnecessary and/or non-functional equipment or items were observed in the retail area (near and above walk-in cooler). Establishments shall be kept free of unnecessary and/or non-functional equipment and litter to prevent harborage conditions. Repeat Violation. Correct By: 10/29/2024
Fresh potato and ginger were observed unwholesome to sight. Food was voluntarily disposed of. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 10/15/2024

Raw animal foods such as eggs were observed stored above ready-to-eat foods such as milk in the reach-in refrigerator. Raw animal foods must be separated from raw ready-to-eat food such as vegetables during storage. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 10/15/2024

1. The presence of cockroaches is not controlled as evidenced by live insect observed during time of inspection and deceased cockroaches observed on glue trap. 2. The presence of rodents is not controlled as evidence by rodent droppings on the floor and live rodent observed on glue trap. The presence of insects, rodents, and other pests must be controlled by routinely inspecting the premises for evidence of pests, using acceptable methods to control pests, eliminating harborage conditions, and routinely inspecting incoming shipments of food and supplies. Deceased pests must be removed at a frequency to prevent attractants for other pests. Glueboards were removed during time of inspection. Establishment must work with commercial pest control services to eliminate pest issues. New Violation. Correct By: 10/15/2024

The door to the outside, located in the front, is not tight-fitting. Outer openings of a food establishment must be protected against the entry of insects and rodents by tight-fitting doors. New Violation. Correct By: 10/29/2024
The non-food contact surfaces of the open air case have an accumulation of residue, and other debris. New Violation. Correct By: 10/29/2024

Unnecessary and/or non-functional equipment or items were observed in the retail area (in corner near office and near and above walk-in cooler). Establishments shall be kept free of unnecessary and/or non-functional equipment and litter to prevent harborage conditions. New Violation. Correct By: 10/29/2024

The ceiling tiles in the deli area are constructed of composite acoustic tiles. This material is not durable. Materials for wall surfaces shall be smooth, durable, easily cleanable, and non-absorbent for areas subject to moisture such as food preparation areas, walk-in refrigerators, ware-washing areas, and toilet rooms. New Violation. Correct By: 01/30/2025

Food contact surfaces of slicer were observed with an accumulation of food and debris. The food contact surfaces of equipment must be clean to sight and touch. Slicer to be cleaned and sanitized prior to use. Repeat Violation. Correct By: 08/07/2024

The cutting board on table in the deli area is very scratched and scored. Cutting blocks and boards that are subject to scratching and scoring shall be resurfaced if they can no longer be effectively cleaned and sanitized, or discarded if they are not capable of being resurfaced. New Violation. Correct By: 08/13/2024

The ceiling tiles in the deli area are constructed of composite acoustic tiles. This material is not durable. Materials for wall surfaces shall be smooth, durable, easily cleanable, and non-absorbent for areas subject to moisture such as food preparation areas, walk-in refrigerators, ware-washing areas, and toilet rooms. New Violation. Correct By: 01/30/2025

Management's plan for assessing, monitoring, and controlling foodborne disease hazards is inadequate. The Person in charge did not assure compliance with critical code requirements. New Violation. Correct By: 07/30/2024

The hand-washing sink in the deli area was blocked by a fan. A hand-washing sink shall be accessible at all times for employee use. Fan was relocated at time of inspection. Soap was not available at the hand-washing sink in the kitchen. Hand-washing sinks must be provided with a supply of hand cleaning liquid, powder, or bar soap. Soap was provided during time of inspection. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 07/30/2024

Various milks, cream, and cheeses were observed unwholesome to sight. Milks and cream were observed curdled. Cheeses were observed with mold. Baby formula was observed for retail sale exceeding expiration date. All unwholesome and expired foods were voluntarily disposed of during time of inspection. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 07/30/2024

Food contact surfaces of the bandsaw, slicer, and meat tenderizer were observed with an accumulation of food and debris. The food contact surfaces of equipment must be clean to sight and touch. Food contact surfaces must be cleaned and sanitized before use. New Violation. Correct By: 07/30/2024

Various foods were observed held at 45-54 degrees Fahrenheit in deli display case and curtained open air case. Time/temperature control for safety (TCS) food must be held at 41 degrees Fahrenheit or below except during preparation, cooking, or cooling. Eggs and milk under 45 degrees and wholesome to sight were moved to other refrigeration during time of inspection. Raw chicken was moved to freezer during time of inspection. Foods that were out of temperature for an unknown amount of time were voluntarily disposed of. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 07/30/2024
Cheeses were observed for retail sale beyond use by and sell by dates. Foods were voluntarily disposed of during time of inspection. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 07/30/2024

Working containers used for storing chemicals taken from bulk supplies were not marked with the common name of the chemical. Chemical was labelled during time of inspection. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 07/30/2024

The following equipment was observed in poor repair: 1. curtained open air case (ambient 47 degrees Fahreheit, taken out of service durig time of inspection) 2. walk-in cooler (taken out of service, prior to inspection) Equipment to be repaired/ refurbished/ replaced. New Violation. Correct By: 08/06/2024

The cutting board on table in the deli area is very scratched and scored. Cutting blocks and boards that are subject to scratching and scoring shall be resurfaced if they can no longer be effectively cleaned and sanitized, or discarded if they are not capable of being resurfaced. New Violation. Correct By: 08/13/2024
The non-food contact surfaces of the deli display case and curtained open air case have an accumulation of soil residue and other debris. New Violation. Correct By: 08/06/2024

The ceiling tiles in the deli area are constructed of composite acoustic tiles. This material is not durable. Materials for ceiling surfaces shall be smooth, durable, easily cleanable, and non-absorbent for areas subject to moisture such as food preparation areas, walk-in refrigerators, ware-washing areas, and toilet rooms. New Violation. Correct By: 01/30/2025

The establishment does not employ a full-time, on-site manager certified in food safety at all times when time temperature control for safety (TCS) food is being prepared, handled, and/or served. New Violation. Correct By: 01/02/2024

Ambient temperature at the walk-in refrigeration unit was observed held at 49 degrees Fahrenheit. No TCS food items were observed stored on unit at the time of the inspection. Time/temperature control for safety (TCS) food must be held at 41 degrees Fahrenheit or below except during preparation, cooking, or cooling. Repeat Violation. Correct By: 01/02/2024

Unnecessary and/or non-functional equipment or items were observed in the retail area. Establishments shall be kept free of unnecessary and/or non-functional equipment and litter to prevent harborage conditions Repeat Violation. Correct By: 01/16/2024

Employees' clothing or other personal belongings were observed stored in the KITCHEN area. Lockers or other suitable facilities shall be used for the orderly storage of employees' clothing and other possessions. Repeat Violation. Correct By: 01/02/2024
Management's plan for assessing, monitoring, and controlling foodborne disease hazards is inadequate. New Violation. Correct By: 12/22/2023

An employee was observed touching ready to eat food such as tortillas with bare hands. Food employees must not touch exposed, ready-to-eat food with their bare hands and shall use suitable utensils such as deli tissue, spatulas, single use gloves, or dispensing equipment. vOLUNTARILY DISPOSED COMPLETED. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 12/22/2023
The hand-washing sink in the kitchen was blocked by cutting board. A hand-washing sink shall be accessible at all times for employee use. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 12/22/2023
The hand-washing sink in the kitchen was lacking paper towels. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 12/22/2023

Staff failed to sanitize equipment and utensils after washing and rinsing them. After being cleaned, equipment and utensils must be sanitized in the 3-bay-sink or dishwasher. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 12/22/2023

soup, beef stew, rice, fried tacos, fried pork, and beans were observed held between 64-120 degrees Fahrenheit at the steam line table and over the stove. Time/temperature control for safety (TCS) food must be maintained at 135 degrees Fahrenheit or above except during preparation, cooking, or cooling. Some food items were reheated and moved a different steam table for active hot holding. Spanish Hot Holding Food Temperature Log New Violation. Correct By: 12/22/2023

Ambien temperature of the walk-in unit, deli unit and produce unit were observed held at between 62-64 degrees Fahrenheit. Time/temperature control for safety (TCS) food must be held at 41 degrees Fahrenheit or below except during preparation, cooking, or cooling. Food items were store on unit were voluntarily disposed of. Service was called during inspection. Spanish Cold Holding Food Temperature Log New Violation. Correct By: 12/22/2023

Rice, deli meats and cooked chicken were observed not marked with the date of preparation at the refrigeration unit and preparation unit. Ready-to-eat time/temperature control for safety (TCS) foods prepared in the establishment and held for more than 24 hours must be marked with the date of preparation. Food items were voluntarily disposed of. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 12/22/2023
Shredded Chicken was observed stored on unit and holding for more than 7 days. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 12/22/2023

There is insufficient hot holding equipment to maintain foods at 135 degrees F or above. Unit was put out of service during inspection. Unit was observed in poor repair not holding temperature more than 112 degrees F. New Violation. Correct By: 01/05/2024

Thermometers are lacking in the walk-in refrigeration unit(s). Cold holding equipment used for time/temperature control for safety (TCS) foods must have at least one thermometer that is located to allow easy viewing of the temperature display. New Violation. Correct By: 12/22/2023
Drain 3 bay sink stopers are in poor repair or missing. New Violation. Correct By: 01/05/2024
The facility is lacking test strips to measure the concentration of the chlorine sanitizing solution for the 3 bay sink. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 12/22/2023
The non-food contact surfaces of the deli-unit refrigeration have an accumulation of dust, dirt, food residue, and other debris. New Violation. Correct By: 12/22/2023

Unnecessary and/or non-functional equipment or items were observed in the retail area. Establishments shall be kept free of unnecessary and/or non-functional equipment and litter to prevent harborage conditions. New Violation. Correct By: 01/05/2024

Employees' clothing or other personal belongings were observed stored in the kitchen/prep-area. Lockers or other suitable facilities shall be used for the orderly storage of employees' clothing and other possessions. New Violation. Correct By: 12/22/2023
